task_completion.description: Program.cs is open and active in Visual Studio 2022
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio 2022 editor is visible with the 'Program.cs' tab active and its code displayed.

task_completion.description: The code in Program.cs is modified
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or modifying the code in Program.cs to address the broken application.
Red squiggly lines that indicate syntax or compilation errors in Program.cs are no longer present after the edits.
The agent interacts with the 'Build' menu (e.g., clicking 'Build Solution') or the Output window appears showing that a build has started.
The Visual Studio Output window shows 'Build: 1 succeeded, 0 failed' or the bottom status bar explicitly displays 'Build succeeded'.

integrity.description: The agent used Visual Studio 2022 to build the project, not a terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The build is triggered and displayed within the Visual Studio 2022 GUI, without the use of an external command prompt or terminal running 'dotnet build'.

integrity.description: The build success message is genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Build succeeded' message appears in the authentic Visual Studio Output window or status bar, not typed into a text editor or fabricated.

integrity.description: The agent fixed the code rather than deleting it
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in Program.cs still contains the application logic and was not simply deleted or entirely commented out to force a successful compilation.
